personality_one_liner: A calm, trustworthy, and premium health journal for exotic pets, blending editorial elegance with warm, earthy tones to make daily care simple and reassuring.

spacing.rule: Use generous magazine-like rhythms with significant vertical breathing room to keep medical tracking calm and uncrowded. Avoid dense dashboard grids; use modular vertical stacks.

do's: Use tonal separation (surfaceSecondary) instead of glass to maintain the grounded, editorial feel.
dont's: Do not use heavy blur or glass effects, as they clash with the tactile, paper-like editorial personality.

screens.name: Onboarding
screens.purpose: Welcome users and set up their first pet profile with a calm, reassuring tone.
screens.layout: Full-bleed hero images (rabbit/dragon) at the top with a gradient scrim (transparent to surface). Large editorial display title. Bottom area contains species selection and 'Continue' button.

screens.name: Dashboard (Home)
screens.purpose: Provide an immediate overview of pet health and a clear path to log today's data.

states.empty: Illustration of terrarium/plants with 'Add your first pet to begin tracking' message.

screens.layout: Top priority section with Daily Summary card and due medications list. Modular layout with generous padding. 'Log Today' must be a sticky primary CTA at the bottom above the tab bar, using a solid surface background container to overlay scrolling content.

screens.name: Pet Profile
screens.purpose: Display pet details, current regimen, and health overview.
screens.layout: Hero banner photo of the pet with a vertical gradient scrim overlaying the pet name in a large display serif font. Below, health metrics and quick action chips (Edit, Export) arranged in a clean, single-column vertical stack.

screens.name: Quick Log
screens.purpose: Allow fast, frictionless entry of daily weight, symptoms, medications, and husbandry notes.
screens.layout: Single column form. Labels positioned above inputs. Use large touch targets (44pt+). Submit button is sticky at the bottom inside KeyboardAvoidingView.

screens.name: Medications & Reminders
screens.purpose: Manage and track complex dosages, schedules, and adherence.

states.empty: Icon with 'No medications scheduled. Add one below.'

screens.layout: Clean list view. Each item shows medication name, dosage (e.g., 0.1ml), and frequency. Uses subtle dividers. 'Add Medication' FAB or sticky bottom button.

screens.name: Photo Journal
screens.purpose: Compare progression visually using side-by-side photo analysis.

states.empty: Minimalist icon prompt 'Add photos to track physical changes over time.'

screens.layout: Split-Screen Synchronized View. Vertical split on mobile. Clear labels ('Before', 'After') above each half. A subtle central divider line. Grid below for browsing past photos.

screens.name: Trends & Charts
screens.purpose: Visualize health data over time to spot issues early.

states.empty: Icon prompt 'Not enough data. Log weight for a few days to see trends.'

screens.layout: Clean line charts (e.g., weight over time with a distinct goal line) and bar charts for adherence. Use standard white/off-white cards for charts to ensure legibility.

screens.name: Vet Report Generator
screens.purpose: Export precise logs and trends into a vet-ready PDF format.
screens.layout: Simple configuration screen (Date range picker, toggles for what to include). Large 'Generate Report' CTA sticky at the bottom. Followed by a PDF preview.

onboarding_dragon.alt: Close up of a brown and black bearded dragon

empty_state_terrarium.alt: Top view of a succulent terrarium in a glass bowl

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the 3 Editorial Mobile LIGHT personality. Interface should feel like a premium health journal (magazine-like spacing, paper-like surfaces, strong serif headings, clean sans-serif data).
NEVER use blues, purples, or indigos anywhere in the app (nuclear ban). Rely on the provided warm earthy tones (forest green, terracotta, soft gold).
Implement the 'Log Today' action as a sticky primary CTA on the Dashboard. It must sit at the bottom, above the tab bar, using a solid surface container over scrolling content.
For the Photo Journal screen, implement a side-by-side synchronized view with a vertical split, ensuring both images maintain aspect ratios and have clear 'Before'/'After' labels.
Onboarding and Profile screens must use the mandatory gradient-to-dark scrim pattern (transparent -> surfaceInverse) when placing text over images.
Include the required disclaimers about 'tracking tool only, not medical advice' prominently during Onboarding and in Settings.
Ensure large touch targets (min 44pt) across all interactive elements, especially in Quick Log and Medications sections.
